message:
refactor(frankenterm-core): scaffold StorageHandle impl-split with event-mute beachhead (ft-aw52a / ft-dn2tu Phase 6)

Phase 6 of the ft-dn2tu storage.rs library-vs-binary split — the
deferred-by-design "P3 cleanup pass" that the bead author flagged for
"AFTER Phases 1-5 land". This commit lays the scaffolding rather than
attempting a full 5K-line move that would saturate a verification
cycle.

What landed:

- New `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage/handle/` directory with a
  `mod.rs` that documents the per-feature impl-split model and lists
  the ~12 follow-up clusters (audit, segments, events, mutes, panes,
  workflows, sessions, reservations, fts/search, timeline,
  retention/cleanup, lifecycle/init).
- `storage/handle/event_mutes.rs` carries the beachhead extraction:
  one `impl StorageHandle { … }` block with the eight cohesive async
  event-mute methods (`add_event_mute`, `remove_event_mute`,
  `is_event_muted`, `list_active_mutes` + each `_with_cx` sibling),
  ~108 LOC. Chosen because the group is small and references exactly
  four upstream items (`EventMuteRecord`,
  `WriteCommand::{UpsertEventMute, DeleteEventMute}`,
  `PooledReadConn`, `Result`).
- `mod handle;` declared inside `storage.rs` so the handle subtree is
  a descendant of `storage::*` — that lets event_mutes.rs reach
  StorageHandle's private fields (`write_tx`, `db_path`, …) and
  private methods (`recv_writer_response`, `spawn_blocking_storage`)
  without widening any visibility on the type itself.
- Storage.rs's giant `impl StorageHandle` block (lines 2698-2804)
  loses the eight event-mute methods, replaced by a stub-pointer
  comment.

What's intentionally NOT in this commit:

- The remaining ~282 methods in the giant impl. Each cluster ships
  as its own follow-up bead so the splits stay reviewable. Per the
  bead's P3 framing, this is cleanup work, not a unitary move.

Discovery note: the bead's "46 impl blocks scattered across the rest
of the file" turned out to be one giant `impl StorageHandle` block
post-Phase-1-through-5 — a previous agent appears to have already
consolidated. That makes the per-feature split cleaner than the bead
anticipated: each cluster is just a continuous slice of the giant
block.

Verification:

- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` → clean
  (1m16s).
- `cargo check --workspace` → frankenterm-core itself clean; two
  upstream system-level build errors (openssl-sys, tikv-jemalloc-sys)
  surfaced from frankenterm-mux-server / frankenterm-dynamic
  pre-existed in this environment and are unrelated to the
  StorageHandle move.
- Submodule descendant relationship verified: `mod handle;` in
  storage.rs gives event_mutes.rs access to StorageHandle privates
  without any pub-widening.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+//! [ft-aw52a / ft-dn2tu Phase 6] StorageHandle impl-split scaffolding.
+//!
+//! Phase 6 of the storage.rs library-vs-binary split. The
+//! `pub struct StorageHandle` definition stays in `storage.rs`
+//! alongside the writer-thread loop and the WriteCommand enum. Its
+//! ~290 public async methods are being progressively split into
+//! per-feature submodules so future readers don't have to hold the
+//! entire 30k-line file in their head.
+//!
+//! ## Scaffolding model
+//!
+//! Each per-feature file holds a single `impl StorageHandle { ... }`
+//! block carrying its method group. Because these submodules sit
+//! under `storage::handle::*`, they are descendants of `storage` and
+//! can therefore access StorageHandle's private fields
+//! (`write_tx`, `db_path`, etc.) without widening their visibility.
+//!
+//! ## Beachhead
+//!
+//! - [`event_mutes`] — 8 async methods governing persistent event
+//!   mute records (`add_event_mute`, `remove_event_mute`,
+//!   `is_event_muted`, `list_active_mutes` + their `_with_cx`
+//!   siblings). ~108 LOC. Chosen as the beachhead because the group
+//!   is small, cohesive, and references exactly four public types
+//!   (`EventMuteRecord`, `WriteCommand::{UpsertEventMute,
+//!   DeleteEventMute}`, `PooledReadConn`, `Result`).
+//!
+//! ## Remaining work (filed as follow-ups)
+//!
+//! The other ~282 methods in the giant `impl StorageHandle` at the
+//! top of `storage.rs` group naturally into roughly 12 feature
+//! clusters (audit, segments, events, mutes, panes, workflows,
+//! sessions, reservations, fts/search, timeline, retention/cleanup,
+//! lifecycle/init). Each cluster is its own follow-up bead so the
+//! splits ship as small reviewable chunks rather than a monolithic
+//! 5K-line move that would drop a verification cycle on the floor.
+
+mod event_mutes;
